content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`
Document lines: 93-167

### 3.1 `Source`

Сырой источник знания.

Назначение:
- хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records
- быть первичным evidence layer
- служить опорой для re-check и explainability

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `source_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id: text`
- `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record`
- `conversation_id: text | null`
- `turn_id: text | null`
- `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external`
- `author_label: text | null`
- `content_text: text`
- `content_hash: text`
- `observed_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `metadata_json: jsonb`
- `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported`
- `expires_at: timestamptz | null`
- `deleted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `embedding: vector | null`

Инварианты:
- source не должен silently изменяться
- `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time
- `content_hash` полезен для dedupe
- delete policy должна быть явной

---

### 3.2 `Entity`

Канонический узел памяти.

Назначение:
- объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта
- быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes
- поддерживать aliases и merge

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `entity_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service`
- `canonical_name: text`
- `normalized_name: text`
- `summary: text | null`
- `status: active | merged | deleted`
- `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `attributes_json: jsonb`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`

Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`:
- `alias_id`
- `entity_id`
- `alias_text`
- `alias_normalized`
- `source_id`
- `confidence`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- merge не удаляет историю
- alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity
- ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail

---

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`Source` as immutable evidence layer with explicit lifecycle controls summary=In 02. Data Model section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer for chat/tool/imported/external records, intended for re-check and explainability. Its invariants require no silent mutation, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce an explicit delete policy. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,source,invariants,schema evidence=Инварианты: - source не должен silently изменяться - `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time - `content_hash` полезен для dedupe - delete policy должна быть явной
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`Entity` canonicalization and reversible merge policy via aliases and audit trail summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with `entity_aliases` as a separate table for alias tracking. Invariants state that merges must preserve history, aliases must point to the current canonical entity, and wrong merges must be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<2>,entity:<3> keywords=Entity,entity_aliases,canonical entity,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,invariants,aliases evidence=Инварианты: - merge не удаляет историю - alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity - ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 Data Model layered architecture and cross-cutting concerns summary=The `v3` memory model is intentionally split into layers: evidence (`Source`), semantic graph (`Entity`, `Claim`), intermediate semantics (`Note`), operational projections (`Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`), and audit (`MemoryRun`), while applying two cross-cutting concerns: `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle`. This defines the core architectural direction and component roles. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3,data model,Source,Entity,Claim,Note,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,scope,retrieval lifecycle tags=architecture,memory_model,layering useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Scope envelope and truth-vs-retrieval lifecycle separation in 02. Data Model summary=The document prescribes dual scope representation (`scope_text` for human explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), and explicitly separates `truth status` from `retrieval_state` as independent axes. Retrieval lifecycle follows `active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`, with usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`) supporting maturity, retention, a...[truncated 10 chars] scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,truth status,retrieval_state,active,warm,cold,archived,deleted,use_count,last_used_at,last_validated_at tags=schema_pattern,retrieval,lifecycle,governance useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.93,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Adds distinct, section-specific invariants and schema intent for Source beyond existing high-level architecture note.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.93,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Adds distinct, section-specific Entity canonicalization, alias table, and reversible merge invariants not captured in active notes.","target_note_id":null}]}
